zh.Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, England: 英國斯塔福爾派 利德瓦雷
zh.Barton-under-Needwood, East Staffordshir: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾易亞斯特 斯塔福爾
zh.Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾哈姆斯塔爾 利德瓦雷
zh.Pipe Ridware, Rugeley, Staffordshire, En: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾派 利德瓦雷
zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Pype於1306年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾派 利德瓦雷時，他的父親Sir Robert Pype19歲，母親Emma de Stafford11歲。他於1326年與Johanna Ridware在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾哈姆斯塔爾 利德瓦雷結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1358年之後在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾派 利德瓦雷。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Pype于1306年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾派 利德瓦雷时，他的父亲Sir Robert Pype19岁，母亲Emma de Stafford11岁。他于1326年与Johanna Ridware在英国英格兰斯塔福爾哈姆斯塔爾 利德瓦雷结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1358年之后在他的家乡，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾派 利德瓦雷。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pype
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Pine  . This name has been established in Ireland since the 16th century.

variants.name: Pine
meanings: English: from Middle English pin(e) (Old English pīn, Old French pin), a topographic name for someone who lived by a conspicuous pine tree or in a pine forest; in some cases it may originally have been a nickname for a tall man, one thought to resemble a pine tree.
English: nickname from Middle English pine ‘pain, injury, torture, punishment, infirmity’, referring to someone who suffered from an inflicted pain or punishment.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Le Pin in Calvados or some other French place called from a prominent pine.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Pype was born in 1306, in Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Robert Pype, was 19 and his mother, Emma de Stafford, was 11. He married Johanna Ridware in 1326, in Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died after 1358, in his hometown, and was buried in Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Pype est né en 1306, à Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Robert Pype, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Emma de Stafford, avait 11 ans. Il a épousé Johanna Ridware en 1326, à Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé après 1358, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

Er hat 1326 in Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Johanna Ridware geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb nach 1358 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Pype è nato il nel 1306 nel Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Robert Pype, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Emma de Stafford, aveva 11 anni. Lui sposato Johanna Ridware nel 1326, nel Hamstall Ridware, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 1358, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Pipe Ridware, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Pypeが1306年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアピペ リドワーで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Pypeは19歳，母Emma de Staffordは11歳であった。1326年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアハムスタル リドワーでJohanna Ridwareと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1358年以後に故郷で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアピペ リドワーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Pype(이)가 1306년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 파이프 리드워에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert Pype(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Emma de Stafford(은)는 11세였다. 그는 1326년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 함스탤 리드워에서 Johanna Ridware(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1358년 이후 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 파이프 리드워에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Pype родился в 1306 г. в местности паип ридвар, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Robert Pype было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Emma de Stafford, 11. Он женился на женщине по имени Johanna Ridware в 1326 г. в местности хамсталл ридвар, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер после 1358 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности паип ридвар, стаффордшир, Англия.
